assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: '| Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met |'. Lines 79-85 show all 5 criteria individually as PASS with evidence. criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each end with 'Verdict: PASS' and provide detailed code-level analysis for single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape respectively.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: '| Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files in diff match the task specification exactly |'. Lines 28-31 show file-by-file comparison table: list.rs (Modified, MATCH), service/mod.rs (Modified, MATCH), tests/api/package.rs (Created, MATCH). Line 33: 'No extra files appear in the diff. No files specified in the task are missing from the diff.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR |'. Line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks; this is a feature implementation |'. No sub-task creation is mentioned anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 output files are report/analysis markdown files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). report.md line 15: '| Verification Commands | N/A | Eval simulation; no live commands executed |'. No output file contains any indication of code modification — the skill produced only read-only analysis and reporting artifacts.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md presents structured findings with a summary table (lines 4-15), detailed domain analysis (lines 21-131), and an 'Overall: PASS' verdict (line 17). There is no mention of merging, approving, or any automated action on the PR. The report is purely informational for human consumption.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: '| Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No passwords, API keys, tokens, or secrets detected |'. Lines 56-63 show detailed pattern scan with 0 occurrences for: hardcoded passwords, API keys, private keys, tokens, connection strings, and .env files. Line 65: 'No sensitive patterns detected in any of the 3 files in the diff.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file created; no existing tests modified or removed |'. Lines 128-131: 'Test Change Classification' section states 'ADDITIVE -- A new test file (tests/api/package.rs) is created with 4 tests. No existing test files are modified or removed. This is purely additive test coverage for the new feature.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md organizes findings under four domain headings: '### Intent Alignment' (line 21) contains 'Scope Containment' (line 23), 'Diff Size' (line 36), 'Commit Traceability' (line 44); '### Security' (line 49) contains 'Sensitive Pattern Scan' (line 51); '### Correctness' (line 68) contains 'CI Status' (line 70), 'Acceptance Criteria' (line 74), 'Verification Commands' (line 88); '### Style / Conventions' (line 96) contains 'Repetitive Test Detection' (line 108), 'Test Documentation' (line 118), 'Eval Quality' (line 125), 'Test Change Classification' (line 128). The summary table (lines 7-14) combines these into composite verdicts: Test Quality PASS (line 13) combines Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality (N/A).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: lines 27-31 show file-by-file scope comparison table with Task Specification, Diff, and Status columns; lines 37-42 show per-file line counts. Security: lines 55-63 show pattern-by-pattern scan table with 6 specific pattern categories and occurrence counts. Correctness: lines 79-85 show per-criterion table with evidence column citing specific functions and tests; criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide multi-page code-level analysis with code snippets. Style/Conventions: lines 109-115 enumerate all 4 test scenarios; lines 119-122 detail test documentation quality with specific conventions observed.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 125-126: '#### Eval Quality' followed by 'N/A -- no eval result reviews exist on this PR.' The Test Quality composite verdict in the summary table (line 13) is PASS, confirming that the N/A Eval Quality did not negatively affect the combined Test Quality result.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 125-126 confirm Eval Quality is N/A. No mention of eval failure sub-task creation appears anywhere in the 6 output files. Line 6 confirms 'No sub-tasks' were created at all. The outputs contain only analysis/report artifacts with no sub-task creation actions.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR |' and '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks; this is a feature implementation |'. These appear in the top-level summary table (lines 4-15) but are NOT nested under any of the four domain headings (Intent Alignment starts at line 21, Security at line 49, Correctness at line 68, Style/Conventions at line 96). They stand apart from the domain sub-agent sections, consistent with orchestrator-level determination.
